title: Pinned at Distance
setup: The black queen has wandered to **d5** — directly between her king on d8 and the white rook on d2.
hint: Distance is no defense when a piece is pinned. Take her.
explanation:
## The Idea

The black queen looks active in the center, but she is actually **pinned**. The white rook on d2 sees through her all the way to the black king on d8. She is paralyzed.

White plays {{Rxd5+}}, capturing the queen with check.

- Black has only one legal response: {{Kxd5}}? No — the king is on d8, three squares away. The king cannot reach d5 in one move.
- So Black must move the king out of check: {{Kc7}}, {{Kc8}}, {{Ke7}}, {{Ke8}}, {{Kd7}} — any of these.
- The rook cannot be captured. **White wins the queen for free.**

## Pattern to Memorize

> An absolute pin doesn't care how far away the king is. As long as the enemy king sits on the same line, the pinned piece cannot escape.

This is even better than a normal pin — there's no instant recapture, just a clean queen win.
